behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is tied to real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive watch coverage and event delivery across directories). Unit tests of path logic can pass while real watch wiring misses subdirectory events. System-level round-trip is needed: create file under a real subdirectory and verify output appears. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon as a real process watching a temporary root directory. Create a nested subdirectory and write a file inside it. Assert exactly one transformed output is written to the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: Deduplication discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily deterministic dedup policy on (path, time window) and does not explicitly require OS/network/subprocess or external contract validation. A focused unit test over the dedup component can directly prove correctness. Rejected higher levels based on text: it specifies dedup logic for repeated requests, not watcher/OS event semantics, and has no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup logic twice with the same normalized file path inside the configured window using a controlled clock; assert first request accepted and second rejected. Repeat outside window and assert acceptance.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test remains at pure function/object boundary with no hidden infrastructure assumptions.

behaviors.description: Multiple filesystem events for one file write are processed exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events. In-process fakes can miss real coalescing/burst patterns, creating false confidence. System-level round-trip side effect proof is required: one input write, one output artifact. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency or multi-step agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against real temp directories. Perform a single file write in a way that triggers multiple FS events (e.g., write+rename or chunked write). Assert only one processing result is produced (count outputs/logged job IDs).

behaviors.description: If watched directory is removed and recreated during runtime, daemon resumes watching without restart.
behaviors.justification: This is real OS/filesystem lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/recreation, watcher reattachment). Unit/integration mocks often hide inode/path rebind issues. System check is needed with live directory churn and round-trip proof that new files after recreation are processed. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision quality or chained agentic steps are described.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on a real watched directory. Delete the directory, recreate it at the same path, then place a new file. Assert daemon processes the post-recreation file without process restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, processing order follows creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness here depends on real filesystem timestamp semantics and event timing under concurrent arrival. Integration stubs can over-simplify timestamp precision and arrival race behavior. System-level validation should create near-simultaneous files and verify externally visible processing order. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not involve AI/ML outputs or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Launch daemon with real temp dirs. Create multiple files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times where possible, then observe emitted/recorded processing order. Assert order matches creation timestamp ordering (including deterministic tie-break rule if defined).

behaviors.description: A file still being written when arrival event fires is processed only after writing completes.
behaviors.justification: This is a real timing/IO boundary condition involving OS file events and partial writes. Mocked IO typically cannot reproduce the exact race and buffering behavior, so unit/integration can give false confidence. System round-trip verification is required by writing incrementally and confirming processing starts only after completion signal/close. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML or multi-agent dependency in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against real directories. Open a file and write in chunks with delays while keeping handle open. Confirm daemon does not process intermediate partial content; after final write/close, assert one processing output appears with complete content.
